tasks.description: Provision all infrastructure resources required by AlertHub: PostgreSQL for relational data, Redis/Valkey for caching and rate limiting, Kafka for event streaming, MongoDB for flexible document storage, RabbitMQ for task queues, and SeaweedFS for object storage. This foundational task must complete before any backend services can be deployed.
tasks.details:
1. Deploy PostgreSQL cluster using CloudNative-PG operator:
   - Create Cluster CR with 1 instance, 20Gi storage on mayastor
   - Initialize 'alerthub' database with 'alerthub_user' owner
   - Configure max_connections=200, shared_buffers=256MB
   - Create tables: users, tenants, notifications, rules, audit_logs
   - Connection string: postgresql://alerthub_user:<password>@alerthub-postgres-rw.databases.svc:5432/alerthub

2. Deploy Redis/Valkey using Redis Operator:
   - Create Redis CR with valkey:7.2-alpine image
   - Allocate 5Gi persistent storage on mayastor
   - Configure resource limits: 500m CPU, 1Gi memory
   - Connection string: redis://alerthub-valkey.databases.svc:6379

3. Deploy Kafka cluster using Strimzi operator:
   - Create Kafka CR with version 3.8.0, 1 replica
   - Configure topics: alerthub.notifications.created (6 partitions), alerthub.notifications.delivered (3 partitions), alerthub.notifications.failed (3 partitions)
   - Set retention: 7 days for created/delivered, 14 days for failed
   - Bootstrap servers: alerthub-kafka-kafka-bootstrap.kafka.svc:9092

4. Deploy MongoDB using Percona operator:
   - Create PerconaServerMongoDB CR with version 7.0.14-8
   - Configure rs0 replica set with 1 member, 10Gi storage
   - Create collections: integrations, templates, delivery_logs
   - Connection string: mongodb://alerthub-mongodb-rs0.databases.svc:27017

5. Deploy RabbitMQ using RabbitMQ operator:
   - Create RabbitmqCluster CR with 1 replica, 10Gi storage
   - Configure queues with DLQ: integration.slack.delivery, integration.discord.delivery, integration.email.delivery, integration.webhook.delivery
   - Connection string: amqp://alerthub-rabbitmq.messaging.svc:5672

6. Configure SeaweedFS for object storage:
   - Create S3 buckets: alerthub-attachments, alerthub-exports, alerthub-media
   - S3 endpoint: http://seaweedfs-filer.seaweedfs.svc:8333

7. Create shared ConfigMap 'alerthub-config' with all connection strings

8. Create Secrets for database credentials, API keys

subtasks.description: Provision PostgreSQL database cluster using CloudNative-PG operator with configured storage, connection limits, and initial database schema for AlertHub relational data.
subtasks.details: Create CloudNative-PG Cluster CR with 1 instance and 20Gi mayastor storage. Configure max_connections=200, shared_buffers=256MB. Initialize 'alerthub' database with 'alerthub_user' owner. Create tables: users, tenants, notifications, rules, audit_logs. Generate connection string: postgresql://alerthub_user:<password>@alerthub-postgres-rw.databases.svc:5432/alerthub. Apply CR using kubectl and verify pod status and readiness probes.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate PostgreSQL pod is running, test connection using psql client, verify database and tables exist, check storage provisioning
subtasks.title: Deploy PostgreSQL Cluster with CloudNative-PG Operator

subtasks.description: Provision Redis/Valkey instance using Redis Operator for caching and rate limiting with persistent storage and resource limits configured.
subtasks.details: Create Redis CR using valkey:7.2-alpine image with 5Gi mayastor persistent storage. Configure resource limits: 500m CPU, 1Gi memory. Set up connection string: redis://alerthub-valkey.databases.svc:6379. Apply CR and verify pod deployment, storage binding, and service endpoint availability.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify Redis pod running, test connection with redis-cli, validate storage persistence, check resource limits applied
subtasks.title: Deploy Redis/Valkey Cluster with Redis Operator

subtasks.description: Provision Kafka cluster using Strimzi operator with configured topics, partitions, and retention policies for event streaming.
subtasks.details: Create Kafka CR with version 3.8.0 and 1 replica. Configure topics: alerthub.notifications.created (6 partitions), alerthub.notifications.delivered (3 partitions), alerthub.notifications.failed (3 partitions). Set retention: 7 days for created/delivered, 14 days for failed. Bootstrap servers: alerthub-kafka-kafka-bootstrap.kafka.svc:9092. Apply CR and verify Kafka broker pods, Zookeeper pods, and topic creation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate Kafka and Zookeeper pods running, verify topics created with correct partitions using kafka-topics.sh, test producer/consumer connectivity
subtasks.title: Deploy Kafka Cluster with Strimzi Operator

subtasks.description: Provision MongoDB replica set using Percona operator for flexible document storage with configured collections and storage allocation.
subtasks.details: Create PerconaServerMongoDB CR with version 7.0.14-8. Configure rs0 replica set with 1 member and 10Gi storage. Create collections: integrations, templates, delivery_logs. Generate connection string: mongodb://alerthub-mongodb-rs0.databases.svc:27017. Apply CR and verify MongoDB pods, replica set status, and collection initialization.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify MongoDB pods running, test connection with mongosh, validate replica set status, check collections exist
subtasks.title: Deploy MongoDB Cluster with Percona Operator

subtasks.description: Provision RabbitMQ cluster using RabbitMQ operator with configured queues, dead letter queues, and storage for task queue management.
subtasks.details: Create RabbitmqCluster CR with 1 replica and 10Gi storage. Configure queues with DLQ: integration.slack.delivery, integration.discord.delivery, integration.email.delivery, integration.webhook.delivery. Generate connection string: amqp://alerthub-rabbitmq.messaging.svc:5672. Apply CR and verify RabbitMQ pods, queue creation, and management UI accessibility.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify RabbitMQ pods running, test connection with rabbitmqadmin, validate queues and DLQs exist, check management plugin
subtasks.title: Deploy RabbitMQ Cluster with RabbitMQ Operator

subtasks.description: Configure SeaweedFS S3-compatible buckets for storing attachments, exports, and media files with proper access configuration.
subtasks.details: Create S3 buckets using SeaweedFS S3 API: alerthub-attachments, alerthub-exports, alerthub-media. Configure S3 endpoint: http://seaweedfs-filer.seaweedfs.svc:8333. Use s3cmd or AWS CLI to create buckets and verify accessibility. Set appropriate bucket policies and access controls.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify buckets created using s3cmd ls, test file upload/download operations, validate S3 endpoint connectivity
subtasks.title: Configure SeaweedFS S3 Buckets for Object Storage

subtasks.description: Create ConfigMap with all connection strings and Secrets for database credentials, API keys to enable backend services to connect to infrastructure components.
subtasks.details: Create ConfigMap 'alerthub-config' containing all connection strings: PostgreSQL, Redis/Valkey, Kafka bootstrap servers, MongoDB, RabbitMQ, SeaweedFS S3 endpoint. Create Secrets for: PostgreSQL password, Redis password (if applicable), MongoDB credentials, RabbitMQ credentials, S3 access keys. Apply ConfigMap and Secrets to appropriate namespace. Ensure proper labeling and annotations for management.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify ConfigMap and Secrets created with kubectl get, validate all connection strings are present and correctly formatted, check secret values are base64 encoded
subtasks.title: Create Shared ConfigMap and Secrets for Infrastructure Connectivity

subtasks.description: Perform comprehensive connectivity validation across all infrastructure components and verify inter-service communication across namespaces.
subtasks.details: Execute connectivity tests from test pods to validate: PostgreSQL connection and query execution, Redis/Valkey connection and cache operations, Kafka producer/consumer operations, MongoDB connection and CRUD operations, RabbitMQ message publishing and consumption, SeaweedFS S3 operations. Verify cross-namespace service discovery and DNS resolution. Test connection strings from ConfigMap. Validate all operators are healthy and CRs are in ready state. Document any connectivity issues or configuration adjustments needed.
subtasks.testStrategy: Deploy test pods with database clients, execute connection tests for each service, verify message flow through Kafka and RabbitMQ, test S3 operations, validate all services accessible via connection strings in ConfigMap
subtasks.title: Validate Infrastructure Connectivity and Integration Testing

tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify PostgreSQL cluster status: kubectl get cluster alerthub-postgres -n databases (should show 1/1 ready)
2. Test PostgreSQL connection: psql -h alerthub-postgres-rw.databases.svc -U alerthub_user -d alerthub -c 'SELECT 1'
3. Verify Redis connectivity: redis-cli -h alerthub-valkey.databases.svc ping (should return PONG)
4. Check Kafka topics: kubectl exec -it alerthub-kafka-kafka-0 -n kafka -- bin/kafka-topics.sh --list --bootstrap-server localhost:9092
5. Test MongoDB connection: mongosh mongodb://alerthub-mongodb-rs0.databases.svc:27017 --eval 'db.runCommand({ping:1})'
6. Verify RabbitMQ queues: kubectl exec -it alerthub-rabbitmq-server-0 -n messaging -- rabbitmqctl list_queues
7. Test SeaweedFS S3 API: aws s3 ls s3://alerthub-attachments --endpoint-url http://seaweedfs-filer.seaweedfs.svc:8333
8. Validate all resources have PersistentVolumeClaims bound
9. Check operator logs for any errors during provisioning
tasks.title: Setup AlertHub Infrastructure (Bolt - Kubernetes)

tasks.description: Build the high-performance core service that receives, validates, and routes notifications. Handles rate limiting, priority queuing, deduplication, and publishes events to Kafka for downstream processing. Provides WebSocket connections for real-time updates and Prometheus metrics for observability.

subtasks.description: Set up the Rust project structure with Cargo.toml containing all required dependencies (axum, tokio, sqlx, redis, rdkafka, serde, uuid, chrono, validator, tracing, prometheus, tower-http). Configure workspace structure and create initial module files (lib.rs, main.rs, models.rs, db.rs, kafka.rs, rate_limit.rs, routes.rs, metrics.rs, websocket.rs).
subtasks.details: Create Cargo.toml with exact versions specified in requirements. Set up src/ directory structure with module declarations. Configure Rust edition 2021, add [profile.release] optimizations for performance (lto = true, codegen-units = 1). Create empty module files with proper mod declarations in lib.rs. Add .cargo/config.toml for build optimizations. Include basic project metadata (name, version, authors).
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify project compiles with `cargo build`, check all dependencies resolve correctly, ensure module structure is valid
subtasks.title: Initialize Rust project with Cargo.toml and core dependencies

subtasks.description: Create comprehensive data models in src/models.rs including Channel enum, Priority enum, NotificationPayload, CreateNotificationRequest with validator derives, and Notification struct with sqlx::FromRow. Implement all serialization/deserialization traits and validation rules.
subtasks.details: Define Channel enum (Slack, Discord, Email, Push, Webhook) with serde rename_all lowercase. Define Priority enum (Critical, High, Normal, Low). Create NotificationPayload with title, body, optional metadata HashMap. Implement CreateNotificationRequest with validator::Validate for tenant_id length validation. Create Notification struct with sqlx::FromRow derive for database mapping. Add custom validation methods for payload size limits and metadata validation. Include conversion methods between request and database models.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for serialization/deserialization, validation rule tests, conversion method tests
subtasks.title: Implement data models with validation and serialization

subtasks.description: Build the database abstraction layer in src/db.rs with sqlx PgPool configuration, connection pool management, and CRUD operations for notifications. Implement insert_notification, get_notification_by_id, and update_notification_status functions with proper error handling.
subtasks.details: Create create_pool function with PgPoolOptions configured for max_connections=50, connection timeout, and idle timeout. Implement insert_notification with prepared statement using sqlx::query! macro for type safety. Add get_notification_by_id with Option<Notification> return. Implement update_notification_status for status transitions. Add batch operations for bulk inserts if needed. Include transaction support for atomic operations. Implement connection health checks and retry logic for transient failures.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests with testcontainers-rs for PostgreSQL, test connection pool exhaustion scenarios, verify query performance
subtasks.title: Implement PostgreSQL database layer with connection pooling

subtasks.description: Create rate limiting module in src/rate_limit.rs using Redis with ConnectionManager. Implement sliding window rate limiting algorithm with configurable limits per tenant, atomic increment operations, and automatic key expiration.
subtasks.details: Create RateLimiter struct wrapping redis::aio::ConnectionManager. Implement check_limit method using Redis INCR and EXPIRE commands atomically. Use time-bucketed keys (rate_limit:{tenant_id}:{window_timestamp}) for sliding window. Add configurable max_requests and window Duration parameters. Implement get_remaining_quota method for API responses. Add support for different rate limit tiers (per tenant configuration). Include error handling for Redis connection failures with circuit breaker pattern.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests with redis-test crate, verify rate limit enforcement across window boundaries, test concurrent request handling
subtasks.title: Implement Redis-based rate limiting with sliding window

subtasks.description: Build Kafka integration layer in src/kafka.rs with FutureProducer configuration, event publishing functions, and robust error handling. Support multiple topics for different notification channels with partitioning by tenant_id.
subtasks.details: Create create_producer function with ClientConfig for bootstrap.servers, message.timeout.ms, compression.type=snappy, and idempotence=true. Implement publish_event with FutureRecord including key (tenant_id) for partition assignment. Add publish_batch for bulk operations. Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for transient failures. Create NotificationEvent wrapper struct for Kafka payloads with metadata. Add topic routing logic based on Channel enum. Include delivery report handling and metrics for success/failure rates.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests with testcontainers Kafka, verify message delivery and ordering, test retry mechanisms
subtasks.title: Implement Kafka producer with event publishing and retry logic

subtasks.description: Create deduplication module in src/dedup.rs using Redis for storing notification fingerprints. Implement content-based hashing to detect duplicate notifications within configurable time windows per tenant.
subtasks.details: Create Deduplicator struct with Redis ConnectionManager. Implement generate_fingerprint function using SHA256 hash of (tenant_id, channel, title, body). Create check_duplicate method that checks Redis SET with TTL (default 5 minutes). Use Redis SET NX for atomic check-and-set operations. Add configurable deduplication windows per priority level. Implement cleanup for expired fingerprints. Include metrics for deduplication hit rate. Add bypass mechanism for Critical priority notifications.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for fingerprint generation, integration tests for duplicate detection, verify TTL expiration behavior
subtasks.title: Implement deduplication logic with Redis-based fingerprinting

subtasks.description: Build comprehensive REST API in src/routes.rs with Axum router, request handlers for notification CRUD operations, validation middleware, error handling, and CORS configuration. Include health check and metrics endpoints.
subtasks.details: Create AppState struct containing PgPool, Redis ConnectionManager, FutureProducer, RateLimiter, and Deduplicator. Implement create_notification handler with validation, rate limit check, deduplication, database insert, and Kafka publish pipeline. Add get_notification handler with path parameter extraction. Implement list_notifications with pagination and filtering. Create health_live (always returns 200) and health_ready (checks DB/Redis/Kafka connectivity). Add CORS middleware with tower-http. Implement request tracing middleware. Create custom error types with IntoResponse implementations for consistent error responses. Add request ID generation and propagation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests for all endpoints, test error scenarios, verify middleware execution order
subtasks.title: Implement Axum route handlers with middleware stack

subtasks.description: Create WebSocket handler in src/websocket.rs for real-time notification delivery. Implement connection management, tenant-based subscription filtering, broadcast mechanisms, and heartbeat/reconnection logic.
subtasks.details: Implement websocket_handler using axum::extract::WebSocketUpgrade. Create WebSocketManager struct with Arc<RwLock<HashMap<TenantId, Vec<WebSocketSender>>>> for connection tracking. Implement subscribe/unsubscribe logic based on tenant_id from query parameters or JWT. Add broadcast_to_tenant function for sending notifications to all connected clients. Implement ping/pong heartbeat mechanism (30s interval). Add graceful connection cleanup on disconnect. Create message types for subscription management and notification delivery. Include connection limits per tenant. Add metrics for active connections and message delivery rates.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests with tokio-tungstenite client, test connection lifecycle, verify broadcast delivery to multiple clients
subtasks.title: Implement WebSocket real-time notification system

subtasks.description: Create comprehensive metrics collection in src/metrics.rs using prometheus crate. Instrument all critical paths with counters, histograms, and gauges. Expose /metrics endpoint for Prometheus scraping.
subtasks.details: Create MetricsRegistry struct wrapping prometheus::Registry. Define metrics: notification_requests_total (counter by channel, priority, status), notification_processing_duration_seconds (histogram with p50/p95/p99), rate_limit_rejections_total (counter by tenant), deduplication_hits_total (counter), kafka_publish_duration_seconds (histogram), websocket_connections_active (gauge by tenant), database_query_duration_seconds (histogram by operation). Implement metrics() handler returning prometheus text format. Add middleware for automatic request duration tracking. Create custom macros for simplified metric recording. Include metric labels for tenant_id, channel, priority for detailed observability.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for metric recording, verify metric format output, test label cardinality limits
subtasks.title: Implement Prometheus metrics instrumentation

subtasks.description: Implement main application entry point in src/main.rs with Tokio runtime setup, dependency initialization, Axum server configuration, signal handling for graceful shutdown, and structured logging with tracing-subscriber.
subtasks.details: Initialize tracing-subscriber with JSON formatting and configurable log levels from environment variables. Load configuration from environment variables (DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, KAFKA_BROKERS, SERVER_PORT). Create database pool, Redis connection manager, Kafka producer, rate limiter, and deduplicator. Initialize WebSocketManager and MetricsRegistry. Build AppState and create router. Configure Axum server with graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT. Implement connection draining with timeout. Add startup health checks for all dependencies. Include panic handler with structured logging. Set up tokio runtime with worker thread configuration based on CPU cores.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests for server startup/shutdown, verify graceful shutdown behavior, test configuration loading
subtasks.title: Create main.rs with server initialization and graceful shutdown

subtasks.description: Build optimized multi-stage Dockerfile for Rust compilation and runtime. Create Kubernetes Deployment, Service, ConfigMap, and HorizontalPodAutoscaler manifests with proper resource limits, health checks, and observability configuration.
subtasks.details: Create Dockerfile with builder stage using rust:1.75-slim, copy Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock, build dependencies separately for layer caching, then copy source and build release binary. Runtime stage using debian:bookworm-slim with only binary and required shared libraries. Set non-root user. Create k8s/deployment.yaml with 3 replicas, resource requests (500m CPU, 512Mi memory) and limits (1000m CPU, 1Gi memory), liveness probe on /health/live, readiness probe on /health/ready with initialDelaySeconds=10. Create k8s/service.yaml as ClusterIP exposing ports 8080 (HTTP) and 9090 (metrics). Create k8s/configmap.yaml for environment variables. Create k8s/hpa.yaml targeting 70% CPU utilization with min=3, max=10 replicas. Add PodDisruptionBudget for high availability. Include ServiceMonitor for Prometheus Operator integration.
subtasks.testStrategy: Build Docker image and verify size (<50MB), test Kubernetes deployment in kind cluster, verify HPA scaling behavior
subtasks.title: Create multi-stage Dockerfile and Kubernetes manifests

tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for models, validation, and business logic:
   - Test Channel and Priority enum serialization
   - Test NotificationPayload validation
   - Test rate limiter logic with mock Redis

2. Integration tests with test containers:
   - Spin up PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka containers
   - Test POST /api/v1/notifications returns 202 with valid ID
   - Test rate limiting blocks after threshold
   - Test deduplication prevents duplicate notifications
   - Test Kafka event publishing
   - Test WebSocket connection and message delivery

3. Load testing with k6:
   - Sustained 10,000 notifications/minute
   - Verify p95 latency < 100ms
   - Test concurrent WebSocket connections (1,000+)

4. End-to-end tests:
   - Submit notification via API
   - Verify record in PostgreSQL
   - Verify event in Kafka topic
   - Verify WebSocket clients receive update

5. Health check validation:
   - GET /health/live returns 200
   - GET /health/ready returns 200 when all dependencies available
   - GET /metrics returns Prometheus format
tasks.title: Implement Notification Router Service (Rex - Rust/Axum)

tasks.description: Build the integration service that delivers notifications to external channels (Slack, Discord, email, webhooks) using Effect TypeScript for type-safe error handling, composable services, and robust retry logic. Consumes events from Kafka via Effect Stream and processes delivery tasks from RabbitMQ with backpressure management.

subtasks.description: Set up the Bun project structure with package.json, tsconfig.json, and install all required dependencies including Effect, Elysia, Drizzle ORM, Kafka, RabbitMQ, and integration libraries. Configure Effect's module resolution and type system settings.
subtasks.details: Create project directory structure (src/, src/services/, src/repositories/, src/consumers/, src/routes/, src/schemas/). Initialize package.json with all dependencies from requirements. Configure tsconfig.json with strict mode, ESNext target, and Effect-compatible settings. Set up .env.example with required environment variables (MONGODB_URL, KAFKA_BROKERS, SMTP credentials). Install dependencies using Bun and verify Effect TypeScript compilation works correctly.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify all packages install without conflicts, tsconfig compiles successfully, and basic Effect imports work
subtasks.title: Initialize Bun project with Effect TypeScript dependencies and configuration

subtasks.description: Implement comprehensive Effect Schema definitions in src/schemas.ts including Channel types, integration configurations (Slack, Discord, Email, Webhook), Integration entity, NotificationMessage, and all tagged error classes with proper validation rules.
subtasks.details: Create src/schemas.ts with Schema imports from effect. Define Channel literal type with all supported channels. Implement SlackConfig, DiscordConfig, EmailConfig, and WebhookConfig schemas with URL pattern validation, port ranges, and email format validation. Create Integration schema with union type for configs. Define NotificationMessage schema with priority levels and nested payload structure. Implement tagged error classes: SlackDeliveryError, DiscordDeliveryError, EmailDeliveryError, RateLimitError, and ConfigurationError with appropriate fields.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit tests validating schema parsing, rejection of invalid data, and proper error class instantiation
subtasks.title: Define Effect Schema definitions for all domain models and error types

subtasks.description: Build SlackService in src/services/slack.service.ts using Effect Context.Tag pattern with exponential backoff retry policy, jittered schedules, and proper error mapping to SlackDeliveryError. Format Slack block kit messages from notification payloads.
subtasks.details: Create SlackService as Effect Context.Tag with deliver method signature. Implement SlackServiceLive Layer using HttpClient from @effect/platform. Transform NotificationMessage into Slack block kit format with markdown text sections. Use HttpClient.request.post with jsonBody for webhook delivery. Implement retry policy with Schedule.exponential starting at 1 second, composed with Schedule.recurs(3) and Schedule.jittered for randomization. Map HTTP errors to SlackDeliveryError with status codes. Handle rate limiting with proper backoff.
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock HTTP client to test retry behavior, error mapping, and successful delivery scenarios
subtasks.title: Implement Slack delivery service with Effect retry logic and error handling

subtasks.description: Build DiscordService in src/services/discord.service.ts following the same Effect pattern as SlackService with Discord-specific webhook payload formatting, retry logic, and DiscordDeliveryError handling.
subtasks.details: Create DiscordService as Effect Context.Tag with deliver method. Implement DiscordServiceLive Layer using HttpClient. Format NotificationMessage into Discord webhook payload with embeds structure (title, description, color, timestamp). Use HttpClient.request.post for webhook URL delivery. Implement identical retry policy as Slack service with exponential backoff and jitter. Map HTTP errors to DiscordDeliveryError. Handle Discord rate limits (5 requests per 2 seconds per webhook).
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock HTTP client to verify Discord payload format, retry behavior, and rate limit handling
subtasks.title: Implement Discord delivery service with webhook integration

subtasks.description: Build EmailService in src/services/email.service.ts using nodemailer with Effect.tryPromise pattern. Integrate Handlebars for email template rendering and implement proper SMTP connection pooling with error handling.
subtasks.details: Create EmailService as Effect Context.Tag with deliver method. Implement EmailServiceLive Layer using Effect.gen to initialize nodemailer transporter. Create SMTP transporter with host, port, and auth from EmailConfig. Implement Handlebars template compilation for email body formatting. Use Effect.tryPromise to wrap sendMail async operation. Map SMTP errors to EmailDeliveryError. Handle connection failures, authentication errors, and recipient validation. Implement connection pooling for performance.
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock nodemailer to test email formatting, SMTP error handling, and template rendering
subtasks.title: Implement Email delivery service with SMTP and template rendering

subtasks.description: Build WebhookService in src/services/webhook.service.ts with HMAC signature generation for secure webhook delivery, custom header support, and retry logic for failed deliveries.
subtasks.details: Create WebhookService as Effect Context.Tag with deliver method. Implement WebhookServiceLive Layer using HttpClient. Generate HMAC-SHA256 signature from webhook secret and payload body. Add custom headers from WebhookConfig including signature header (X-Webhook-Signature). Use HttpClient.request.post with jsonBody containing full notification payload. Implement retry policy with exponential backoff. Handle webhook endpoint failures and timeout errors. Support both HTTP and HTTPS URLs with proper TLS validation.
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock HTTP client to verify signature generation, custom headers, and retry behavior
subtasks.title: Implement Webhook delivery service with signature verification

subtasks.description: Build IntegrationRepository in src/repositories/integration.repository.ts using MongoDB driver wrapped in Effect.tryPromise for all CRUD operations with proper error mapping and connection management.
subtasks.details: Create IntegrationRepository as Effect Context.Tag with methods: findById, findByTenant, create, update, delete. Implement IntegrationRepositoryLive Layer using Effect.gen to connect to MongoDB. Wrap all MongoDB operations (findOne, find, insertOne, findOneAndUpdate, deleteOne) in Effect.tryPromise with error mapping to Error type. Use Schema.decode to validate Integration documents from database. Implement proper index creation for tenantId and channel fields. Handle connection failures and query timeouts gracefully.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests with MongoDB test container verifying all CRUD operations and error scenarios
subtasks.title: Implement MongoDB repository layer with Effect error handling

subtasks.description: Build notification consumer in src/consumers/notification.consumer.ts using Effect Stream to consume from Kafka topic, process messages with backpressure, and coordinate delivery through integration services.
subtasks.details: Create createNotificationConsumer function using Effect.gen. Initialize KafkaJS consumer with groupId 'integration-service'. Subscribe to 'alerthub.notifications.created' topic. Create Stream.async to wrap Kafka eachMessage callback. Parse message value as NotificationMessage using Schema.decode. Use Stream.mapEffect to process each message: fetch integrations from repository, filter by channel and enabled status, select appropriate service (Slack/Discord/Email/Webhook), invoke deliver method. Implement backpressure with Stream.buffer and Stream.throttle. Handle consumer errors with Effect.catchAll and implement reconnection logic.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests with Kafka test container verifying message consumption, backpressure, and error recovery
subtasks.title: Implement Kafka consumer with Effect Stream for notification processing

subtasks.description: Build RabbitMQ consumer in src/consumers/task.consumer.ts using amqplib with Effect wrappers for processing delivery tasks, implementing exponential backoff retries, and dead-letter queue for failed deliveries.
subtasks.details: Create task queue consumer using Effect.gen to connect to RabbitMQ. Declare queue 'integration.tasks' with dead-letter exchange configuration. Wrap amqplib channel.consume in Effect.tryPromise. Parse task messages as NotificationMessage. Implement retry logic: on delivery failure, republish to queue with x-death count header incremented. After 5 retries, move to dead-letter queue 'integration.tasks.dlq'. Use Effect.retry with Schedule.exponential for transient failures. Implement prefetch=10 for concurrency control. Handle connection failures with automatic reconnection.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration tests with RabbitMQ test container verifying retry logic, dead-letter routing, and backpressure
subtasks.title: Implement RabbitMQ task queue integration for retry and dead-letter handling

subtasks.description: Build REST API in src/routes/integration.routes.ts using Elysia with routes for CRUD operations on integrations, test delivery endpoint, and proper Effect Layer composition for dependency injection.
subtasks.details: Create integrationRoutes Elysia instance with prefix '/api/v1/integrations'. Implement POST / for creating integrations with Schema validation. Implement GET / with tenantId query parameter for listing. Implement GET /:id for fetching single integration. Implement PUT /:id for updates. Implement DELETE /:id for deletion. Implement POST /:id/test for test delivery using actual service layers. Use Effect.runPromise to execute Effect programs. Compose layers with Effect.provide for IntegrationRepository and all delivery services. Add error handling middleware to map Effect errors to HTTP responses.
subtasks.testStrategy: API integration tests verifying all endpoints, validation, and proper Effect Layer composition
subtasks.title: Implement Elysia REST API routes for integration management

subtasks.description: Build main application in src/index.ts that composes all Effect Layers, starts Elysia server, initializes Kafka and RabbitMQ consumers, and implements graceful shutdown with resource cleanup.
subtasks.details: Create main Effect program using Effect.gen. Compose all service layers (SlackServiceLive, DiscordServiceLive, EmailServiceLive, WebhookServiceLive, IntegrationRepositoryLive) using Layer.merge. Initialize Elysia app with integrationRoutes and health endpoint. Start Kafka consumer with createNotificationConsumer. Start RabbitMQ consumer for task queue. Implement graceful shutdown with Effect.addFinalizer to close all connections. Use Effect.runPromise to execute main program. Add process signal handlers for SIGTERM and SIGINT. Implement structured logging with Effect.log for all operations.
subtasks.testStrategy: End-to-end tests verifying server startup, consumer initialization, and graceful shutdown
subtasks.title: Implement main server with Effect Layer composition and consumer startup

subtasks.description: Build production-ready Dockerfile using Bun runtime, create Kubernetes Deployment and Service manifests with proper resource limits, health checks, and ConfigMaps for configuration. Document deployment process and Effect patterns used.
subtasks.details: Create multi-stage Dockerfile: build stage with Bun to install dependencies and compile TypeScript, production stage with minimal Bun runtime image. Copy only necessary files and set non-root user. Create k8s/deployment.yaml with replicas=3, resource requests/limits (CPU: 500m/1000m, Memory: 512Mi/1Gi), readiness/liveness probes hitting /health endpoint. Create k8s/service.yaml with ClusterIP type exposing port 3000. Create k8s/configmap.yaml for environment variables. Create k8s/secret.yaml template for sensitive configs. Write README.md documenting Effect patterns, Layer composition, error handling approach, and deployment steps. Include architecture diagram showing Effect dependency graph.
subtasks.testStrategy: Build Docker image and verify it runs correctly, validate Kubernetes manifests with kubectl dry-run
subtasks.title: Create Dockerfile, Kubernetes manifests, and deployment documentation

tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests for Effect services:
   - Test SlackService.deliver with mock HttpClient
   - Test EmailService.deliver with mock SMTP
   - Test error handling and retry logic with Effect.retry
   - Test Effect Schema validation for Integration configs

2. Integration tests with test containers:
   - Spin up MongoDB, Kafka, RabbitMQ containers
   - Test POST /api/v1/integrations creates integration in MongoDB
   - Test GET /api/v1/integrations lists integrations
   - Test POST /api/v1/integrations/:id/test delivers test message
   - Test Kafka consumer processes notification events

3. Effect-specific tests:
   - Test Effect.retry with simulated failures (should retry 3 times)
   - Test Effect.Schema validation rejects invalid configs
   - Test Effect Stream backpressure with high message volume
   - Test Effect error propagation through service layers

4. End-to-end delivery tests:
   - Mock Slack webhook endpoint, verify payload format
   - Test rate limiting with Effect Semaphore
   - Test concurrent deliveries to multiple channels
   - Verify delivery events published to Kafka

5. Performance tests:
   - Process 1,000 notifications/minute sustained
   - Verify Effect Stream handles backpressure correctly
   - Test retry logic doesn't cause memory leaks

6. Error handling tests:
   - Test SlackDeliveryError caught and logged
   - Test RateLimitError triggers backoff
   - Test ConfigurationError fails immediately without retry
tasks.title: Implement Integration Service with Effect (Nova - Bun/Elysia)
